title: Zeugites haitiensis
intro: Zeugites haitiensis, also known as Haitian grass or Guayacán, is a plant species belonging to the family Poaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation, treating fever, and alleviating respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Zeugites haitiensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Zeugites haitiensis is commonly used in herbal preparations such as infusions, teas, and extracts, and is also utilized in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Zeugites haitiensis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Poaceae, commonly known as grasses. It belongs to the subfamily Panicoideae and the tribe Paniceae. This plant is native to the island of Hispaniola, which is shared by Haiti and the Dominican Republic. Zeugites haitiensis is a type of wild grass that grows in the region's tropical and subtropical environments. Further classification of Zeugites haitiensis can be found in botanical resources.
